summary: Matched the common fighter Bowser/Koopa thrown-state slice by moving `ftCo_800BCDE0`, `ftCo_800BCE64`, and the no-op `ftCo_ThrownKoopa*` Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into new C source/header files. The PR also wired the new object into `obj_files.mk`, included the new header from `ftKp_Init.c`, removed stale declarations from `ft_0877.dox` and `ft_08A4.h`, and eliminated the local sdata2 float constants from the assembly file. The report shows the new `ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c` file at 100.00% with all listed functions matched.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c` containing matched C for `ftCo_800BCDE0` and `ftCo_800BCE64` plus empty callback bodies for `ftCo_ThrownKoopaF/B/AirF/AirB` Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll functions.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKoopa.h` with address-commented prototypes for the thrown Koopa helpers and callbacks.
Deleted the corresponding assembly implementations from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, including the `blr`-only callback stubs and two local float constants `ftCo_804D8B90` and `ftCo_804D8B94`.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`, preserving object ordering for the split-out text.
Included `ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKoopa.h` in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKoopa/ftKp_Init.c` so Koopa motion-state table references can use the new prototypes.
Removed the `ftCo_800BCE64` prototype from the broad `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h` header and removed a block of stale/common Koopa capture/thrown declarations from `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.dox`.
smart_moves: Factored common thrown-state setup into a local `static inline void inlineA0(...)` for the first matched function, capturing repeated behavior: copy facing direction from `victim_gobj`, clear `fp->mv.co.capturekoopa.x0`, call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, install `ftCo_800DE508` into `x21B0_callback_Accessory1`, and call `ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF)`.

Kept `ftCo_800BCE64` as an explicit expanded sequence under a `SOLUTION == 1` path with a `@todo Use #inlineA0`, which documents that the inline abstraction is conceptually right but did not currently satisfy matching constraints for that function.
Used `FtMotionId`, `MotionFlags`, `ftCo_GObj*`, and `ftCo_Fighter*` instead of generic `enum_t`/`HSD_GObj*` in the new header and C file, improving type precision for future fighter work.
Preserved all 4-byte callback stubs as empty C functions, matching the original `blr` functions while moving symbol ownership into the ftCommon source file.
Used a `MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused stack array in both nontrivial functions to account for stack layout only when exact matching requires it.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a matched region out of a large assembly file, add the new C object immediately before the residual assembly object in `obj_files.mk` so subsequent symbols retain their expected addresses/order.
Empty state callbacks can be represented directly as empty C functions when the original assembly body is only `blr`; this is useful for Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll state tables.

For motion-state transition helpers, matching may depend on whether a repeated helper is truly inlined. A readable `static inline` can work for one function, but another nearly identical function may still need an expanded body to reproduce stack/register behavior.
Use `MUST_MATCH`-guarded dummy stack locals sparingly and annotate them as unused stack so future cleanup can revisit them when compiler behavior or surrounding types improve.
Moving declarations from broad legacy headers/dox files into a feature-specific header helps isolate common fighter state APIs and reduces reliance on catch-all `ft_08A4.h` declarations.
naming_conventions: Feature-specific common fighter files use the `ftCo_ThrownKoopa` basename under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`.
Thrown Koopa callbacks follow the pattern `ftCo_ThrownKoopa{F,B,AirF,AirB}_{Anim,IASA,Phys,Coll}`.
Unknown address-based helpers remained named by address as `ftCo_800BCDE0` and `ftCo_800BCE64`.
The new header uses an address-style include guard `GALE01_0BCDE0`, matching the first function address in the file.
Header prototypes retain address comments such as `/* 0BCDE0 */`, preserving searchability between C declarations and the original DOL address map.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly loads of `0.0f` and `1.0f` from local sdata2 symbols with direct C float arguments to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`; deletion of `ftCo_804D8B90` and `ftCo_804D8B94` indicates those constants were no longer needed in the residual assembly slice.

`ftCo_800BCDE0` calls `inlineA0(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, 0)` and then `ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj)`, matching the assembly sequence that changed motion state, set callbacks/accessory flags, and then advanced/reset animation via `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.

`ftCo_800BCE64` builds a compound `MotionFlags` mask from `Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim`, `Ft_MF_SkipColAnim`, `Ft_MF_UpdateCmd`, `Ft_MF_SkipItemVis`, `Ft_MF_Unk19`, `Ft_MF_SkipModelPartVis`, `Ft_MF_SkipModelFlags`, and `Ft_MF_Unk27`, corresponding to the immediate flag value in the deleted assembly.
`fp->cur_anim_frame` is passed as the animation start for `ftCo_800BCE64`, matching the deleted assembly load from fighter offset `0x894`.
The empty callback functions intentionally compile to 4-byte `blr` functions, matching the PR report's 100-byte max/0-score entries for each no-op callback.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
Inline code comments in the diff identify two self-review/todo items: `@todo Unused stack.` around `MUST_MATCH` dummy stack arrays and `@todo Use #inlineA0.` in `ftCo_800BCE64`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the `SOLUTION` macro or make `ftCo_800BCE64` use `inlineA0`.
Search for additional `MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays in ftCommon motion-state code to identify shared matching patterns.
Inspect neighboring Koopa capture/wait/damage PRs for the same `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` and `ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF)` setup pattern.
Check whether `GALE01_*` include guards were later standardized or replaced for feature-specific headers.
